subject: Physics
chapter: Exploring the Investigative World of Science

activities_projects.title: Design Your Own Scientific Investigation
activities_projects.objective: Develop the ability to design a complete scientific investigation
steps: Choose a question from daily life (e.g., Which material melts ice fastest?)
Write a hypothesis
Design an experiment with variables and controls
Record observations and conclude results
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the full process of scientific investigation
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of experimental design and conclusion (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Variable Identification Activity
activities_projects.objective: Understand independent, dependent, and controlled variables
steps: Select a simple experiment like plant growth in sunlight
Identify the independent variable
Identify the dependent variable
List controlled variables
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognises different types of variables in experiments

activities_projects.title: Experimental Error Analysis
steps: Observe a simple experiment demonstration
List possible sources of error
Suggest ways to reduce those errors
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Develops critical thinking about experimental accuracy

activities_projects.title: Scientific Method Flowchart
activities_projects.objective: Analyse and represent the scientific method
steps: Study the steps of scientific investigation
Create a flowchart showing each step
Include observation, hypothesis, experiment, and conclusion
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Deep understanding of the scientific method
activities_projects.evaluation: Completeness and clarity of flowchart (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Real-Life Phenomenon Investigation
activities_projects.objective: Apply scientific thinking to explain real-life phenomena
steps: Choose a daily phenomenon (e.g., why dough rises)
Write observations about it
Propose a scientific explanation
Suggest an experiment to test it
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Applies scientific reasoning to everyday life
activities_projects.evaluation: Logical explanation and experiment design (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Observation Data Table Project
steps: Observe a process such as ice melting or plant growth
Record observations in a structured table
Analyse patterns in the data
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Improves scientific data recording and interpretation
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy and organisation of data (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Hypothesis Testing Challenge
activities_projects.objective: Test multiple hypotheses for a scientific question
steps: Choose a scientific question
Write two different hypotheses
Design experiments to test each hypothesis
Predict possible outcomes
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of hypotheses and testing plan (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Controlled Experiment Planning
activities_projects.objective: Understand the importance of controlling variables
steps: Plan an experiment where only one factor changes
List all other factors that must remain constant
Explain why controlling variables is important

activities_projects.title: Scientific Investigation Report
steps: Perform a simple investigation
Write the aim, hypothesis, procedure, results, and conclusion
Present the report clearly
activities_projects.evaluation: Structure and clarity of report (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Compare Observation and Experiment
steps: Choose one natural event to observe
Describe observations without testing
Design an experiment to investigate it further
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands difference between observation and experimentation
